---
title: "Research"
toc: false
---

My research interests are in **discrete, convex, and computational geometry**, with a current focus on polyhedral geometry and unfoldings of convex polyhedra.

## Current research

I am currently working with [Gregory Chambers](https://profiles.rice.edu/faculty/gregory-chambers){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} on problems in discrete and convex geometry, especially questions related to **unfoldings of convex polyhedra**.

Broadly, I am interested in:

- polyhedral geometry;
- edge unfoldings and affine unfoldings of convex polyhedra;
- combinatorial and topological methods in geometry;
- blocking and covering problems for convex bodies;
- computational geometry.

## Previous research

Before joining the Mathematics Department, I worked in the [CMOR Department](https://cmor.rice.edu/){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} at Rice University with [Matthias Heinkenschloss](https://profiles.rice.edu/faculty/matthias-heinkenschloss){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} on parameter identifiability and local solution manifolds in nonlinear least squares problems.

As an undergraduate at the Moscow Institute of Physics and Technology, I worked with [Alexander Polyanskii](https://polyanskii.com/){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} on problems in combinatorial and convex geometry. My bachelor’s thesis studied **blocking numbers of convex bodies**.

## Talks and posters

- **Exploring Local Solution Manifolds of Nonlinear Least Squares Problems**  
  Poster presented at [RTG NASC 2024](https://rtg-nasc.rice.edu/news-and-events/annual-rtg-workshop){target="_blank" rel="noopener"}, Rice University, and the [SIAM TX/LA 2024 Section Annual Meeting](https://math.artsandsciences.baylor.edu/conferences/siam-conference){target="_blank" rel="noopener"}, Baylor University.  
  [Poster](files/posters/LSQSolutionManifold.pdf){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} · [Abstract](files/posters/LSQSolutionManifold-abstract.qmd){target="_blank" rel="noopener"}

## Undergraduate and earlier projects

- **Blocking Numbers of Convex Bodies**  
  Bachelor’s thesis, Moscow Institute of Physics and Technology, supervised by [Alexander Polyanskii](https://polyanskii.com/){target="_blank" rel="noopener"}.

- **Maximum-sum matchings of points**  
  Research experience at MIPT, supervised by Alexander Polyanskii.

- **Rigidity of Laman graphs and the Bellows conjecture**  
  Research practicum at MIPT. I studied problems related to rigidity theory and found a proof of the rigidity of Laman graphs using the theory of places.
